activities_projects.title: Comparison of Forest Types
activities_projects.objective: Understand differences between major forest types in India
steps: Draw a table with two columns
Write features of Tropical Evergreen Forests in one column
Write features of Tropical Deciduous Forests in the other
Add examples of trees found in each forest
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students compare major vegetation types in India
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy and clarity of comparison (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Vegetation Distribution Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Analyze why different vegetation types occur in different regions
steps: Observe the vegetation map in the textbook
Identify areas with evergreen forests and thorn forests
Explain why these regions have different vegetation
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand how climate influences vegetation

activities_projects.title: Forest and Wildlife Relationship Study
activities_projects.objective: Understand the connection between vegetation and wildlife
steps: Choose two types of forests
List animals that live in each forest
Explain how forests support these animals

activities_projects.title: Medicinal Plants Research
activities_projects.objective: Learn about traditional medicinal plants in India
steps: Select three medicinal plants mentioned in the chapter
Write their scientific or local names
Explain their medicinal uses
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the value of plant biodiversity

activities_projects.title: Wildlife Conservation Discussion
activities_projects.objective: Understand the importance of wildlife protection
steps: Discuss threats to wildlife such as hunting and deforestation
Write three reasons for wildlife decline
Suggest two methods to protect wildlife

activities_projects.title: Biosphere Reserve Study
activities_projects.objective: Understand the purpose of biosphere reserves
steps: Write the definition of biosphere reserve
List five biosphere reserves in India
Describe their importance in conservation
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand protected areas in India

activities_projects.title: Forest Resource Utilization Chart
activities_projects.objective: Identify how humans use forest resources
steps: Create a chart showing forest resources
Include wood, medicines, food and raw materials
Explain their uses in daily life
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand economic importance of forests

activities_projects.title: Wildlife Habitat Mapping
activities_projects.objective: Identify habitats of major animals in India
steps: Locate regions where tigers, elephants and rhinoceroses are found
Mark them on the map
Explain why these habitats are suitable
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students link geography with wildlife distribution

activities_projects.title: Human Impact on Forests Study
steps: List human activities causing deforestation
Explain their effects on biodiversity
Suggest solutions to reduce forest loss

activities_projects.title: Wildlife Protection Act Study
activities_projects.objective: Understand government measures for wildlife conservation
steps: Write the year when the Wildlife Protection Act was introduced
Explain its objectives
List two benefits of this act
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand legal protection of wildlife
